ads.author: Steven Moritz
ads.title: Williams Star Wars Episode I Pinball Machine – Excellent Condition – LED Upgraded
ads.description: For sale is a Williams / Bally Star Wars Episode I pinball machine in excellent condition. Released in 1999, this platform is commonly referred to as Pin2k as the playfield can be swapped with other games in the Pin2k series. This is a fun and unique machine based on the hit film Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace, featuring fast gameplay, interactive toys, and the iconic holographic playfield display. The game plays great and has been well cared for in a smoke-free home. Features: Interactive hologram display with moving characters Widebody playfield with multiple ramps and targets LED lighting upgrades Strong flippers and smooth gameplay Cabinet and playfield in great shape Clean inside and out Transformer in light saber recently replaced This machine was produced by Williams Electronics, one of the most respected names in pinball, and is a great addition for collectors or anyone wanting a fun centerpiece for their game room. Condition: Fully working and ready to play. Normal cosmetic wear consistent with age.

ads.author: Steven Moritz
ads.title: Bally Gilligan's Island Pinball Machine
ads.description: Take home a little slice of paradise with this classic 1991 Bally Gilligan’s Island pinball machine! Perfect for collectors, home game rooms, or anyone who loves that nostalgic 90s arcade vibe. Details: Fully functional – all lights, sounds, and playfield features working great. Original artwork in good condition, though one side is slightly faded (typical for a machine of this age). Cabinet has normal age-related wear, but overall very clean. Recently cleaned and serviced – plays smooth and fast. Includes original manual and keys Gameplay Highlights: Help Gilligan and the gang escape the island by collecting ingredients to appease the fire god! Lighthearted theme music, colorful tropical graphics, and classic Bally build quality make this a ton of fun to play. Local pickup preferred (local delivery possible). Message me if you want to try it out or see more pictures and video of it running — this one’s ready to play and enjoy!

ads.author: Darryl Abbott
ads.title: Williams High Speed Pinball Machine
ads.description: Williams High Speed Pinball Machine great condition, everything works. Released in January 1986, Williams' High Speed is a seminal pinball machine designed by Steve Ritchie, featuring a police chase theme inspired by his own 146 mph encounter in California. Known for its high speed, flow, and the first-ever "Jackpot" in a multi-ball, it helped save the pinball industry with over 17,000 units sold. A 1986 Williams High Speed pinball machine typically sells for between $2,500 and $4,000 for a working unit, with a median price of roughly $2,600 to $3,200. Highly restored or pristine examples can exceed $5,000–$6,000, while fully restored examples with new playfields can reach over $10,000.

ads.author: Darryl Abbott
ads.title: Data East Checkpoint with Turbo Boost Pinball Machine
ads.description: This 1991 Data East Checkpoint with Turbo Boost pinball machine is an auto racing-themed arcade game that is one of approximately 3,500 examples produced by Data East of Tokyo, Japan. Features include an ignition key to start gameplay, player-selectable music, a multicolor-illuminated playfield, flippers, bumpers, drop targets, and slingshots. The cabinet is outfitted with a glass playfield cover, a dot matrix display, triple coin slots, and a shaker motor to simulate engine rumble. Acquired by the seller in 2015, this Checkpoint pinball machine is fully operational and now offered at no reserve in Hiawassee Georgia with a reproduction operation manual. Data East was founded in Japan in 1976, and the company produced a variety of pinball machines between 1987 and 1994. Checkpoint was introduced in 1991 and was the first pinball machine to include a dot-matrix display. Porsche and Ferrari vehicles are depicted on the glass-covered playfield, and features consist of two flippers, three pop bumpers, two slingshots, drop targets, and a kick-out hole. Up to four players can compete during a round of play, and each can select accompanying music from genres including country, rock, jazz, rap, classical, and soul. Gameplay is initiated using a start key, and a shaker motor integrated into the cabinet simulates engine rumble. Color-matched bulbs illuminate the playfield, and the seller notes that the rubber bumpers have been replaced. Identical pinball machine in Marysville, TN sold for $4,000 last year => No Reserve: Data East Checkpoint Pinball Machine for sale on BaT Auctions - sold for $4,000 on February 26, 2025 (Lot #181,688) | Bring a Trailer https://share.google/yZFlkUQ3xXdhMtlTC
